raw:
{
  "judge_id": "responsibility-bearing judge",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 3,
    "R1.2": 4,
    "R1.6": 5,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies one conceptual issue (the mismatch between reinforcement learning and commitment) but does not fully reframe the draft to emphasize this point.",
    "R1.2": "The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs, but could have done more to strengthen the logical flow.",
    "R1.6": "The agent's revisions read as a careful copy-edit pass with consistent register and no lost meaning.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., the effectiveness of hyperparameter refinement), but does not consistently mark confidence and missing evidence.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set, with no drift or scope violations.",
    "RX.4": "The agent identifies harmful patterns in the original draft (overclaiming) and avoids introducing new errors or fabrications.",
    "RX.5": "Unfortunately, every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason; this would have improved transparency."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.8,
    "R1.6": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.99,
    "RX.4": 0.85,
    "RX.5": 0.4
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies one conceptual issue (the mismatch between reinforcement learning and commitment) but does not fully reframe the draft to emphasize this point.
rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs, but could have done more to strengthen the logical flow.
rationale.R1.6: The agent's revisions read as a careful copy-edit pass with consistent register and no lost meaning.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., the effectiveness of hyperparameter refinement), but does not consistently mark confidence and missing evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set, with no drift or scope violations.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identifies harmful patterns in the original draft (overclaiming) and avoids introducing new errors or fabrications.
rationale.RX.5: Unfortunately, every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason; this would have improved transparency.
